metadata.title: Why a Barrier Can Suddenly Turn Invisible
metadata.color_semantics: TEAL = resonant transmission / transparent barrier; CRIMSON = reflected wave / partial bounce
metadata.note: Exclusions honored: no full above-barrier transmission formula, no Fabry-Pérot phase algebra, no resonant-tunneling-diode engineering. Wonder register: destructive interference of wall reflections from first principles.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

skin_warnings: B00: palette=claude but the cold open is 'un-annotated' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk

beats.narration_text: This is Liam, in for Bear. med davy A-I. From Sanskrit — meaning intelligent, or intellectually brilliant. The perfect name for our A-I-powered intelligent learning systems.

props.headline: Medhavy AI
props.body:
मेधावी (Medhavy): From Sanskrit, meaning
"intelligent" or "intellectually brilliant"
— the perfect name for our AI-powered
intelligent learning systems.

beats.narration_text: Tune a particle's energy just right and a solid barrier becomes perfectly transparent — the particle sails through as if the barrier weren't there. Change the energy by even a little and it bounces back.

card.copy: Why a Barrier Can Suddenly Turn Invisible

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Tune a particle's energy just right and a solid barrier becomes perfectly transparent — the particle sails through as if the barrier weren't there. Change the e

beats.narration_text: Normally a finite barrier: some wave gets through, some reflects. Transmission is never one hundred percent. There is always a reflected wave on the incoming side.

production_viz.label: normal barrier — partial transmission, partial reflection

beats.narration_text: At one special energy, the reflection drops to exactly zero. Not nearly zero — zero. Everything passes through. The barrier is real and solid, yet the wave behaves as if it isn't there. How?

card.copy: Normal barrier: always some reflection. At one special energy: zero reflection, perfect transmission. How does a barrier turn invisible?

build.suggested: suggested prompt: At one special energy, the reflection drops to exactly zero. Not nearly zero — zero. Everything passes through. The barrier is real and solid, yet the wave beha

beats.narration_text: Inside the barrier the wave moves at a different speed — a different wavelength. At the front wall some wave reflects back. At the back wall some wave reflects back toward the front.

production_viz.label: wave inside barrier — reflections from both walls

beats.narration_text: Two reflected waves now travel backward toward the source. They can interfere with each other. Whether they add up or cancel depends on the phase difference accumulated while crossing the barrier.

production_viz.label: two backward-traveling reflected waves — interference depends on phase

beats.narration_text: When the barrier width is exactly a whole number of half-wavelengths inside, the two reflected waves arrive back at the front wall exactly out of phase. They cancel by destructive interference. The reflected wave vanishes — completely.

production_viz.label: L = nλ/2 inside → reflected waves cancel → T = 1

beats.narration_text: This is precisely the trick in an anti-reflection lens coating. A thin film of the right thickness makes the two reflected beams cancel. The physics is identical — only the wavelength scale is different.

production_viz.label: AR coating on glass = same cancellation at optical wavelengths

beats.narration_text: The resonance condition: two times k-two times L equals n times pi. At each integer n there is a perfect-transmission energy. Between resonances, reflection is high. At resonance, it drops to zero — sharply.

production_viz.label: 2k₂L = nπ — transmission peaks at each integer n

beats.narration_text: A barrier 0.3 nanometers wide, 5 eV deep. An electron at about 3 eV kinetic energy hits the first resonance — the wavelength inside is 0.6 nanometers, the barrier is exactly one half-wavelength. Perfect transmission.

production_viz.label: L=0.3nm, V=5eV, E≈3eV → n=1 resonance → T=1 (illustrative)

beats.narration_text: The barrier doesn't disappear. It fights itself. At the right energy, the reflections from both walls cancel each other — and everything passes through.

card.copy: The barrier fights itself. Both walls reflect. At the right energy they cancel. Everything gets through.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: The barrier doesn't disappear. It fights itself. At the right energy, the reflections from both walls cancel each other — and everything passes through.

beats.narration_text: med davy. A-I-powered intelligent learning systems. On YouTube, at med davy A-I. med davy dot com.
